title: 2 Boston men accused of assaulting man who hit their car while trying to park
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Anthony Shearrion, 41, and Arkie Swinson, 45, charged with assault and battery in Boston.
Incident occurred at Blue Hill Avenue and Abbot Street after victim accidentally hit a parked car.
Victim hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries; suspects held at Suffolk County Jail without bail.

title: 2026 Red Sox preview: 4 questions facing Alex Cora’s club
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox's 2026 season hinges on questions about offense, despite a strong pitching rotation led by Garrett Crochet.
Boston's bullpen, a key strength last year, must maintain performance amid aging concerns for Aroldis Chapman.
Infield defensive improvements are needed after leading MLB with 116 errors, despite outfield defensive prowess.

title: Healey announces kickoff of public safety initiative for 2026 World Cup
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Gov. Healey announces 'Match-Ready Massachusetts' safety initiative for 2026 World Cup games in Foxborough.
Seven World Cup matches at 'Boston Stadium' will occur from June 13 to July 9, 2026.
State receives $76 million in federal funds for security, focusing on transportation and crowd control challenges.

title: Kraft-owned New England Revolution backing White Stadium renovation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: New England Revolution supports White Stadium renovation in Franklin Park, backing a public-private partnership with Boston Legacy FC.
Boston Mayor Wu's $135 million renovation project faces opposition from Josh Kraft and a lawsuit citing constitutional violations.
Revolution argues renovation will revitalize the area and provide community benefits, despite ongoing legal challenges from opponents.

title: Maine man in critical condition after stopping to help stranded driver
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 54-year-old Medford man is in critical condition after being struck by a car on I-95 in Veazie, Maine.
The incident occurred when the man helped an 18-year-old Bangor woman who crashed due to snowy conditions.
Both the helping man and woman were struck by a 19-year-old driver from Old Town, but speed wasn't a factor.

title: Berkshire County man arrested after suspicious early-morning fire in his home
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fire believed to be intentionally set occurred at John Rohane's home in Monterey early Wednesday.
Monterey police and State Police searched for Rohane in nearby areas and arrested him Wednesday evening.
Rohane, 60, was taken to a hospital for reasons unrelated to the fire or his arrest.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Tessa, an 11-year-old Labrador, was rescued from a sinkhole by Falmouth Fire and Rescue on Cape Cod.
Crews used pepperoni and a pole leash to safely extract Tessa from a 12-foot deep and 2-foot wide sinkhole.
The sinkhole formed from a suspected underground pipe leak, and Tessa was unharmed after being rescued.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue in New England is rehabilitating 16 axolotls with missing limbs and bacterial infections.
The axolotls, named after cheeses, were surrendered due to overcrowding; some have already been adopted.
The rescue seeks donations to cover vet expenses and supplies for axolotl rehabilitation and foster care.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein's Love Letters newsletter discusses imperfections in relationships and personal growth.
A kitchen table dent symbolizes friendship for Goldstein, while a burn mark on Yowei Shaw's table represents relationship lessons.
Readers are invited to share photos of personal 'cracks' that represent love, community, or lessons learned.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School opens a 25-station cosmetology salon for student training in hair, skin, and nail care.
The salon allows students to gain hands-on experience by working with public clients under instructor supervision.
Students complete 1,000 training hours at the salon before taking the state licensing exam for professional work.
